introSection: Wave Coaster was a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Takarazuka Garden Fields in Takarazuka, Hyogo, Japan. Opened in 1952, the ride was the first roller coaster built in Japan.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride was the first of Sansei's Jet-Roller Coaster model. This model would later mostly focus on the manufacturer's larger coasters, with the Mini-Roller Coaster now being use for small scale coasters and Wild Mice, making the Wave Coaster the odd one out with its child-friendly stats.

fullText:
Wave Coaster was a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Takarazuka Garden Fields in Takarazuka, Hyogo, Japan. Opened in 1952, the ride was the first roller coaster built in Japan.

The ride was the first of Sansei's Jet-Roller Coaster model. This model would later mostly focus on the manufacturer's larger coasters, with the Mini-Roller Coaster now being use for small scale coasters and Wild Mice, making the Wave Coaster the odd one out with its child-friendly stats.
